mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates persistent labor market expansion, which drives consumer income and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency in top PAYEMS growth quintiles does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, further increases in the effective policy rate raise the equity risk premium and tighten financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or the mean return in triggered instances is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in industrial production jointly signal a stagflationary contraction where earnings compress and risk premiums expand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises month-over-month while INDPRO falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in fewer than 45% of joint-signal months, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a bivariate model versus single-series specifications.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in real retail sales signals exhaustion of consumer demand and precipitates downward earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days; this relationship is absent or reversed in early-cycle recoveries.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales momentum slows as personal savings rates compress in late 2024
failure_condition: In late-cycle months, declining RSAFS is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the directional accuracy of the signal is indistinguishable from a coin flip (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production growth above its 12-month trend signals improving manufacturing margins and capacity utilization, attracting cyclical equity inflows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO accelerates, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.20 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in housing starts reflects strengthening residential investment and construction employment, which raises aggregate demand expectations and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following 3-month HOUST increases are negative on average, or the hit rate for positive returns does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI acceleration above 0.3% in a high Fed funds environment amplifies policy uncertainty and forward real rate expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with inflation volatility
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered observations, or the conditional effect of CPI acceleration given high FEDFUNDS is not significantly different from zero (interaction t-stat p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.5 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumption pullbacks and earnings misses, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the cumulative return following trigger events is not significantly negative (bootstrap p > 0.05).
